summary: No extra deterministic setup is needed. The artifact is a Maven SCM provider that builds/executes local `git` command lines; its runtime dependencies are normal Maven transitive dependencies and it does not require a Docker-backed service or optional test dependency for meaningful coverage.
agent_guidance: Keep tests focused on public Maven SCM/gitexe APIs and local temporary files. For version 1.11.1, `GitTagCommand.createCommandLine` has an added `boolean sign` parameter; pass `false` to preserve the previous unsigned tag behavior, or assert `-s` only when intentionally testing signed tags. Avoid network remotes; command-line construction can use example.invalid URLs.
risks: Tests that actually execute git commands, rather than only constructing command lines, may need local `git init` setup and user identity configuration in a temp repository, but this is test logic rather than repository deterministic setup.

Upstream has test-scoped helpers such as `maven-scm-provider-gittest`, but they are not required for the existing coverage style and should not be added unless the generated tests deliberately depend on those fixtures.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8274 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:1.11.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
